title: Adding Redundant Relationships
breadcrumbs: Design Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Adding Redundant Relationships One possibility to improve the performance of the user view is to add a new relationship. Adding a new relationship can shorten an access path, but it might require extra I/O to keep the database consistent. For example, a user view that shows the materials in a configuration is important for the business. A new relationship, 'MATERIAL is from PART', is added to the relational data model, as shown in the following illustration: Figure 1. Adding Redundant Relationships The new relationship makes the access path from CONFIGURATION to MATERIAL considerably shorter because the MATERIAL_ID attribute is stored redundantly in the entity PART. Extra I/O is required to keep the database consistent when maintaining a PART. Figure 2. New Access Profile and Frames-Within-Frames Using Added Relationship The following table shows the amended access profiles: Table 1. Access Profile Statistics for Added Relationship Access path Via Selected Occurrences (avg.) Access Stress Value >Configuration configuration_id 1 1 Configuration>>Part configuration_id 5 5 Part>Material material_id 1 5 Total Access Stress Rating 11 Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
